channel.title: Algorithms for Competitive Programming
channel.description: The goal of this project is to translate the wonderful resource http://e-maxx.ru/algo which provides descriptions of many algorithms and data structures especially popular in field of competitive programming. Moreover we want to improve the collected knowledge by extending the articles and adding new articles to the collection.

item.title: Longest increasing subsequence
item.description:
Longest increasing subsequence

We are given an array with $n$ numbers: $a[0 \dots n-1]$. The task is to find the longest, strictly increasing, subsequence...

item.title: Breadth First Search
item.description:
Breadth-first search

Breadth first search is one of the basic and essential searching algorithms on graphs.

As a result of how the algorithm works, the p...

item.title: String Hashing
item.description:
String Hashing

Hashing algorithms are helpful in solving a lot of problems.

We want to solve the problem of comparing strings efficiently. The brute forc...

item.title: Number of divisors / sum of divisors
item.description:
Number of divisors / sum of divisors

item.title: Binary Search
item.description:
Binary search

Binary search is a method that allows for quicker search of something by splitting the search interval into two. Its most common applica...

item.title: Segment Tree
item.description:
Segment Tree

A Segment Tree is a data structure that stores information about array intervals as a tree. This allows answering range queries over an array...

item.title: Euler's totient function
item.description:
Euler's totient function

item.title: Binary Exponentiation
item.description:
Binary Exponentiation

Binary exponentiation (also known as exponentiation by squaring) is a trick which allows to calculate $a^n$, where $n$ is a non-nega...

item.title: Depth First Search

Depth First Search

item.title: Treap
item.description:
Treap (Cartesian tree)

A treap is a data structure which combines binary tree and binary heap (hence the name: tree + heap $\Rightarrow$ Treap).
